name: Markdown Preview Enhanced
description: Markdown Preview Enhanced ported to vscode

store_metadata.name: Markdown Preview Enhanced
store_metadata.short_description: Markdown Preview Enhanced ported to vscode

findings.detail: shd101wyy.markdown-preview-enhanced is affected by CVE-2025-65716: Markdown Preview Enhanced vulnerability allows crafted Markdown to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the preview iframe, enabling localhost port scanning and potential data exfiltration.
findings.mitigation_note: Vulnerability requires opening a crafted Markdown file. Risk is limited to dev environments. Update to latest version.

findings.name: Suspicious URL in package.json config: markdown-preview-enhanced.krokiServer
findings.description: Hardcoded URL in config default: markdown-preview-enhanced.krokiServer

findings.detail: webpack@5.88.2 has known vulnerability GHSA-38r7-794h-5758 — webpack buildHttp HttpUriPlugin allowedUris bypass via HTTP redirects → SSRF + cache persistence (fix: >=5.104.0)
webpack@5.88.2 has known vulnerability GHSA-4vvj-4cpr-p986 — Webpack's AutoPublicPathRuntimeModule has a DOM Clobbering Gadget that leads to XSS (fix: >=5.94.0)
webpack@5.88.2 has known vulnerability GHSA-8fgc-7cc6-rx7x — webpack buildHttp: allowedUris allow-list bypass via URL userinfo (@) leading to build-time SSRF behavior (fix: >=5.104.1)

vulns.summary: webpack buildHttp HttpUriPlugin allowedUris bypass via HTTP redirects → SSRF + cache persistence
Webpack's AutoPublicPathRuntimeModule has a DOM Clobbering Gadget that leads to XSS
webpack buildHttp: allowedUris allow-list bypass via URL userinfo (@) leading to build-time SSRF behavior

all_findings.name: Webview innerHTML assignment
all_findings.description: Direct innerHTML assignment with dynamic content (XSS vulnerability)

all_findings.name: Device fingerprinting + exfiltration: hardware/environment profiling + network
all_findings.description: Extension collects device hardware information (CPU, memory, network interfaces) and/or environment variables combined with network access - potential device fingerprinting and environment reconnaissance exfiltration.
all_findings.evidence: User/device fingerprinting + Plaintext HTTP endpoint (not HTTPS)

all_findings.name: Remote Automation Agent: shell exec + local server + remote orchestration
all_findings.description: Extension can execute shell commands AND runs a local server or communicates with a remote orchestration backend. This is the architecture of a remote automation agent - the machine becomes a worker node controlled by an external server.
all_findings.evidence: eval() usage + Local HTTP server creation + Plaintext HTTP endpoint (not HTTPS)

signals.description: No access to credential files, SSH keys, or token stores

risk_classification.summary: Multiple suspicious patterns detected alongside high-risk capabilities. Obfuscation, data exfiltration, or credential theft patterns suggest potentially malicious intent. Manual review strongly recommended.
